mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, real disposable income growth becomes the primary driver of consumer spending, and a sustained decline in RSAFS directly reduces equity valuations by signaling weaker corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in the high-inflation regime, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL falls below 250.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 jobs signals an abrupt cooling in the labor market, triggering algorithmic risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 prices over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following payroll misses, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding real economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns in a walk-forward test, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real disposable income and stable short-term rates amplifies consumer spending power, boosting retail and service sector revenues, which positively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint increase in RSAFS and stability in FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, a consecutive two-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals a structural labor market recovery that reduces recession risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Consecutive payroll gains in high-unemployment periods fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal's predictive power reverses when UNRATE falls below 5.0%.
